changeSet.author: openelis-pathology

preConditions.sqlCheck: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM clinlims.notebook WHERE title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND is_template = true

changeSet.comment: Update page orders to make room for new granular workflow pages
changeSet.sql: UPDATE clinlims.notebook_page SET title = 'Gross Examination', instructions = 'Perform macroscopic examination of specimens with photo documentation. Document specimen appearance, dimensions, weight, color, texture, margins, orientation, and abnormalities. Capture up to 96 gross images per specimen. Record sectioning decisions and number of sections to submit for processing.' WHERE title = 'Sample Processing & Aliquoting' AND notebook_id IN (SELECT id FROM clinlims.notebook WHERE title = 'Pathology Laboratory');

changeSet.author: openelis-pathology

preConditions.sqlCheck: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM clinlims.notebook_page np JOIN clinlims.notebook n ON np.notebook_id = n.id WHERE n.title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND n.is_template = true AND np.title = 'Cassette Setup'

changeSet.comment: Add Cassette Setup page - Page 4 in the pathology workflow

column.name: notebook_id
column.valueComputed: (SELECT id FROM clinlims.notebook WHERE title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND is_template = true)

column.name: instructions
column.value: Create and label cassettes for tissue embedding. Transfer tissue sections into labeled cassettes (perforated plastic containers). Use color coding for specimen type or priority. Document tissue orientation and sectioning notes. Track cassette count per specimen.

column.name: content

changeSet.author: openelis-pathology

preConditions.sqlCheck: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM clinlims.notebook_page np JOIN clinlims.notebook n ON np.notebook_id = n.id WHERE n.title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND n.is_template = true AND np.title = 'Block Creation'

changeSet.comment: Add Block Creation page - Page 5 in the pathology workflow

column.name: notebook_id
column.valueComputed: (SELECT id FROM clinlims.notebook WHERE title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND is_template = true)

column.name: instructions
column.value: Create paraffin blocks from processed cassettes. Embed tissue in paraffin wax with correct orientation. Verify tissue orientation before embedding. Document embedding quality, temperature, and station used. Generate block IDs for traceability.

column.name: content

changeSet.author: openelis-pathology

preConditions.sqlCheck: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM clinlims.notebook_page np JOIN clinlims.notebook n ON np.notebook_id = n.id WHERE n.title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND n.is_template = true AND np.title = 'Slide Preparation'

changeSet.comment: Add Slide Preparation page - Page 6 in the pathology workflow

column.name: notebook_id
column.valueComputed: (SELECT id FROM clinlims.notebook WHERE title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND is_template = true)

column.name: instructions
column.value: Cut and prepare slides from tissue blocks via microtomy. Cut thin sections (typically 3-5 um) using microtome. Mount sections on appropriately labeled glass slides. Document section thickness, blade type, and section quality. Float sections in water bath before mounting.

column.name: content

changeSet.author: openelis-pathology

preConditions.sqlCheck: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM clinlims.notebook_page np JOIN clinlims.notebook n ON np.notebook_id = n.id WHERE n.title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND n.is_template = true AND np.title = 'Slide Staining'

changeSet.comment: Add Slide Staining page - Page 7 in the pathology workflow

column.name: notebook_id
column.valueComputed: (SELECT id FROM clinlims.notebook WHERE title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND is_template = true)

column.name: instructions
column.value: Apply routine and special stains to prepared slides. ROUTINE STAINS: H&E for histology, Pap for cytology, Wright-Giemsa for hematology, Gram for microbiology. SPECIAL STAINS: PAS, Masson Trichrome, Reticulin, Iron, Congo Red, AFB, GMS as indicated. IHC/ICC: Immunohistochemistry for protein markers when needed. Run positive and negative controls with each batch. Document stain quality and batch information.

column.name: content

changeSet.author: openelis-pathology

preConditions.sqlCheck: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM clinlims.notebook_page np JOIN clinlims.notebook n ON np.notebook_id = n.id WHERE n.title = 'Pathology Laboratory' AND n.is_template = true AND np.title = 'Microscopy & Diagnosis'

changeSet.comment: Update Microscopy page to focus on diagnosis (staining moved to separate page)
changeSet.sql: UPDATE clinlims.notebook_page SET instructions = 'Perform microscopic examination and enter diagnosis. Two-phase workflow: INITIAL FINDINGS: Pathologist examines stained slides, documents microscopic findings, cellular features, architectural observations, nuclear features, and records initial diagnostic impression. FINAL DIAGNOSIS: Review initial findings, correlate with clinical history, finalize diagnosis with ICD-O/SNOMED codes, document tumor type, grade, stage, margins, and prognostic factors. Upload slide images as supporting documentation.', last_updated = now() WHERE title = 'Microscopy & Diagnosis' AND notebook_id IN (SELECT id FROM clinlims.notebook WHERE title = 'Pathology Laboratory');
